question.text: Which two institutions were created as a result of the Bretton Woods Agreement?

answer.text: International Monetary Fund (IMF) and World Bank
Bank for International Settlements (BIS) and World Trade Organization (WTO)
Federal Reserve and European Central Bank (ECB)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Bretton Woods Agreement of 1944 established the IMF and the World Bank, which played important roles in rebuilding Europe post-World War II. **The Trap:** BIS was established in 1930, before Bretton Woods. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII tests knowledge of international financial institutions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The IMF focuses on monetary stability while the World Bank focuses on development — both still influence currency markets today.
